name: Push Press
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at about chest height. Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width with an overhand grip and unrack it, stepping back. Position the bar across the front of your shoulders with your elbows slightly in front of the bar. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart. This is the starting position.
Perform a quick, shallow dip by bending your knees and hips slightly while keeping your torso upright. This dip should be no deeper than a quarter squat.
Immediately reverse the dip by driving powerfully through your legs and extending your hips and knees. Use this momentum to help press the barbell overhead.

As your legs reach full extension, press the bar vertically overhead until your arms are fully locked out. Move your head slightly back to allow the bar to travel in a straight path, then return your head to neutral when the bar passes.
Lower the bar back to the front of your shoulders in a controlled manner, bending your knees slightly to absorb the weight. Breathe in as you lower.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
